title: node-postgres Pool, Client Ownership, Transactions, and Shutdown · Type-System Trace: Feed Ts One Client Transaction
summary: Each request creates a pool, transaction statements call pool.query, checked-out clients leak on error, row values are trusted, and process exit races pool timers. Predict the reader decision, external-unknown boundary, Promise and AbortSignal ownership, PostgreSQL transaction, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

At Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts One Client Transaction model · Theory Feed Ts On…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 3723 during a clean emitted-package and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove Pool, checked-out client, transaction connection, row validation, error, and close ownership; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node, network, database, and host effects; then change one PostgreSQL constraint, transaction, migration, upsert, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Run BEGIN, every statement, COMMIT or ROLLBACK on the same checked-out client and prohibit pool.query inside the unit of work.

For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts One Client Transaction model · Theory Feed Ts On…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Queries issued through one Pool automatically share a transaction.
